purpose: Provide a controlled, non-public invite package for beginner-friendly mobile web and PWA pilot testing before public launch or store submission.

officialReferences.name: Google Play open, closed, or internal testing

pilotChannels.audience: internal operators and invited friendly testers
pilotChannels.rule: Use as the first pilot channel because it does not require native signing or store review.

pilotChannels.audience: invited Android Chrome and iOS Safari testers
pilotChannels.rule: Use only after real-device install, standalone launch, and offline fallback evidence are captured.
Do not invite through TestFlight until the iOS Capacitor build, signing, screenshots, and review-safe data are ready.
Do not invite through Google Play testing until the Android build, signing, screenshots, and review-safe data are ready.

responsibilities: pause pilot when P0 issues appear
copy P0 issues into the release blocker register
confirm dry-run mode stays enabled when worker/storage gates are incomplete

testerInviteTemplate.subject: AI video workspace mobile pilot
messageBlocks: This is an invited pilot, not a public launch.
Please open https://www.zkraiflow.top on your phone.
Try to finish one simple task: choose a large button, add a demo product image, generate or paste a prompt, submit a dry-run task, and find the result.
Please do not upload private product photos, passwords, customer files, or sensitive business data.
If you get confused, stuck, or see text overlap, submit feedback from the mobile Settings page.
The test is expected to take 30-45 minutes.

testerSafetyNotice: Use demo or non-sensitive product images only.
Do not enter real platform passwords, payment information, private customer data, or legal documents.
Real video generation may stay disabled during pilot; dry-run behavior is expected until worker and storage gates close.
Feedback may be reviewed by the project owner for product improvement.
Ask the pilot owner to remove your test data if you no longer want it retained.

sessionScript.title: Open the mobile web app
sessionScript.expectedSignal: Tester understands this is an AI video workspace from the first screen.

sessionScript.title: Pick a large beginner action
sessionScript.expectedSignal: Tester can choose a task without reading a manual.

sessionScript.title: Add demo product material
sessionScript.expectedSignal: Upload or file selection works on the phone being tested.

sessionScript.title: Generate or paste a prompt package
sessionScript.expectedSignal: Tester can reach the prompt field and understand the next action.

sessionScript.title: Submit a dry-run task
sessionScript.expectedSignal: Tester sees a created task or clear status instead of a dead end.

sessionScript.title: Find assets, result, support, privacy, AI disclosure, and deletion/export paths
sessionScript.expectedSignal: Tester can find operational and compliance surfaces from mobile navigation.

sessionScript.title: Submit feedback from mobile settings
sessionScript.expectedSignal: Feedback is recorded without private screenshots or logs.

successMetrics.target: at least 3 of 5 testers complete the dry-run path without operator intervention
at least 4 of 5 testers can explain which large button they chose and why
at least 4 of 5 testers can find the feedback form from mobile settings
at least 4 of 5 testers can find privacy, AI disclosure, and account deletion or data rights paths
0 unresolved P0 mobile layout, login, upload, or data-rights issues before expanding the pilot

stopRules: Stop inviting testers if login, upload, data deletion/export, or support feedback fails for two consecutive testers.
Stop inviting testers if screenshots or feedback reveal private data that was not intended for the repository.
Stop PWA testing if Android or iOS install launches the wrong origin, wrong icon, wrong name, or blank offline page.
Stop any native beta preparation if signing credentials, keystores, or review passwords appear in repository evidence.
Keep real generation disabled if persistent storage, worker health, and rollback evidence are incomplete.

feedbackTriage.dailyReview: support-reviewer checks pilot feedback once per test day
feedbackTriage.p0Definition: Tester cannot continue the core path, cannot find data rights/legal/support, or sees a privacy/security issue.
feedbackTriage.p1Definition: Tester can finish but experiences confusing copy, cramped layout, slow step, or unclear status.
feedbackTriage.closeoutRule: A P0 or P1 issue is closed only after the fix is verified on the same channel where it was reported.

evidenceOutputs: store/release-blockers-register.json entries for unresolved P0 or launch-blocking findings
closeoutGates: Pilot owner, support reviewer, and rollback owner are named outside placeholder form fields.
Every invited tester receives the safety notice before testing.
npm run pilot:readiness and npm run release:check pass before invites are sent.
PWA install invites are not sent until production device install smoke evidence exists.
Native beta invites are not sent until Android/iOS toolchains, signing, and native QA are complete.
P0 issues are copied into the release blocker register before more testers are invited.
